title: Earnings call transcript: Riot Platforms Q1 2026 revenue beats, stock rises
text: Riot Platforms exceeded revenue expectations with $167 million against forecasts of $122.26 million. However, the company reported a net loss of $500 million ($1.44 per share), driven largely by non-cash mark-to-market adjustments on Bitcoin holdings of $326.7 million. Despite the earnings miss on EPS, the stock rose 7.7% during regular trading and an additional 2% after hours, closing at $16.30. Management attributed this positive reaction to strategic expansions and operational improvements. AMD expanded its partnership with Riot, exercising an additional 25 megawatt expansion option at Rockdale. The total AMD lease now covers 50 megawatts with a projected $636 million in revenue over the primary 10-year period. The company produced 1,473 Bitcoin in Q1 and achieved a 26% reduction in direct mining costs, bringing the cost per Bitcoin to $44,629. Management noted the deployment of 42.5 exahash and $21 million in power curtailment credits. Riot successfully delivered its first 5 megawatts to AMD and reported $33.2 million in total data center segment revenue, with operating lease revenue showing a robust 91% gross margin during the initial phase.
